initiatives.title: Product Factory Cutover
initiatives.summary: Move Wave from wave-level orchestration into a delivery-aware product factory with durable release and acceptance truth.
initiatives.outcome: Wave 17 establishes the release and acceptance spine that the wider product-factory branch can merge after it completes, after which factory-mas-v2 can move intra-wave execution from serial launch to true multi-agent control-plane scheduling.

releases.title: Factory Core v1
releases.summary: Delivery layer above waves with soft states, adhoc runs, design gates, and machine-facing signal surfaces.

releases.title: Factory MAS v2
releases.summary: Opt-in true intra-wave multi-agent execution with explicit agent graphs, per-agent sandboxes, merge queue authority, invalidation, and reducer-backed ready-set selection.

acceptance_packages.title: Factory Core v1 Acceptance
acceptance_packages.summary: Ship decision bundle for the first product-factory release cut.

acceptance_packages.title: Factory MAS v2 Acceptance
acceptance_packages.summary: Ship decision bundle for the true intra-wave multi-agent release.

risks.title: Wave 17 delivery scope drift
risks.summary: Wave 17 is starting soon, so the cutover branch must not redefine release truth in ways that conflict with the main-line landing.

risks.title: MAS graph contract churn
risks.summary: True intra-wave MAS needs new authored-wave graph, barrier, and resource fields, so the release must keep backward compatibility while the serial contract remains live.

debts.title: Adhoc runtime hardening
debts.summary: The first branch lands isolated adhoc execution and promotion, but the lane still needs wider live-use validation before it should be treated as production-default.

debts.title: Serial runtime compatibility bridge
debts.summary: The live runtime still executes one agent at a time inside each wave, so the MAS release must preserve serial execution as the default while sandbox, merge, and invalidation authority land incrementally.
